memory.summary: I planned to finish analysis first, but a friend is inviting me to lunch. Given my high stress and sleep debt, I might prioritize the social invitation over my original plan.
I worked on the analysis section while friends gathered for lunch, but my low energy and high stress make continuing this task difficult right now.
I cannot find 'assignment_A', contradicting my memory of working on analysis. This new info suggests I may have skipped the task entirely or mislabeled it.
I cannot find 'assignment_A', contradicting my memory of working on analysis. This new info suggests I may have skipped the task entirely or mislabeled it.
I made progress on the analysis section, but since it is due in under an hour and I still haven't finished, I need to prioritize completing this task immediately.
The analysis section deadline passed unfinished at 18:00, confirming my earlier suspicion that I either skipped the task or mislabeled it despite working on 'analysis'.
The reading deadline also passed unfinished, confirming a pattern of missing critical tasks despite working on others.
Working 2 hours yielded only minimal progress, confirming a pattern of missing critical deadlines despite effort.
Missing assignment_A confirms I consistently overlook critical tasks even when working, worsening my stress and sleep debt.
Missing the reading deadline reinforces my pattern of overlooking critical tasks despite effort, increasing stress and sleep debt.
One hour of focused work increased my analysis progress by 0.03, yet persistent sleep debt and stress remain high.
One hour of focused work improved analysis progress, but high stress and sleep debt persist despite consistent effort.
